own_review.point: Ser.readResolve() (Converters.java ~948-988) only switches over ids 0-17; the PR adds FILE_CONVERTER (id 19) and URI_CONVERTER (id 20) as Serializable BuiltInConverters whose writeReplace() emits Ser(id), so deserializing them hits the default branch and throws InvalidObjectException via ConfigMessages.msg.unknownConverterId. PATH_CONVERTER (id 18) has the same pre-existing gap; the PR perpetuates the bug instead of fixing it.
documentation/src/main/docs/config/getting-started.md lists the built-in converters (including java.nio.file.Path) but is not updated to mention java.io.File and java.net.URI.

Trimming inconsistency / behavior change: URI_CONVERTER wraps with newTrimmingConverter (previously the implicit ConstructorConverter would throw IllegalArgumentException-wrapped URISyntaxException for ' http://localhost'), while FILE_CONVERTER does not trim (consistent with PATH_CONVERTER but inconsistent with Boolean/numeric/InetAddress/Pattern converters). Tests lock in this asymmetry; worth an explicit decision on whether File should trim too.

points.point: Ser.readResolve missing cases 18/19/20 causing deserialization failure
points.why: Verified: switch ends at case 17, default throws unknownConverterId; BuiltInConverter is Serializable with writeReplace -> Ser(id).

points.point: File converter lacks trimming, inconsistent with most converters; consider trimming
points.why: Verified real inconsistency and actionable; aligns with human reviewer's suggestion to follow the trimming pattern.

missed: Docs (getting-started.md built-in converter list) not updated for File and URI

points.why: Verified blocker, same as v2/v4; correct fix proposed.

points.point: File/Path skip trimming while URI trims; design is sound and tests validate it
points.why: Accurate description but explicitly non-actionable endorsement of the diff.

points.point: Tests are well-structured with good coverage

missed: Docs (getting-started.md built-in converter list) not updated for File and URI

points.point: Critical: Ser.readResolve missing serialization cases for ids 18/19/20
points.why: Verified blocker; correctly notes id 18 is a pre-existing gap and gives the exact fix.

points.point: Consider trimming for File converter for consistency
points.why: Real, actionable, matches the human reviewer's trimming-pattern suggestion; fairly notes Path also doesn't trim.

missed: Docs (getting-started.md built-in converter list) not updated for File and URI

qualitative: All three versions found the single genuinely critical issue: BuiltInConverter is Serializable via Ser(id), and the Ser.readResolve switch tops out at id 17, so the new File (19) and URI (20) converters — like the pre-existing Path (18) — blow up on deserialization with unknownConverterId. v2 and v4 tie for best: both pair the critical serialization finding with the actionable File-trimming inconsistency that echoes the human reviewer's comment, with v4 being the most concise and v2 padding with one praise point. v3 is weakest — after the serialization finding, its remaining two points are non-actionable endorsements of the diff and the tests. Nobody fabricated anything; all line references and behavior claims check out against the code. The shared miss is the documentation: getting-started.md enumerates the built-in converters (including Path) and should gain File and URI entries.
